components.name: sodium acetate
components.evidence: One liter of medium contained 7.5 g (NH4)2SO4, 14.4 g KH2PO4, 0.5 g MgSO4·7H2O, 27.3 g sodium acetate, 2 mL trace metals solution, 1 mL vitamin solution, and 1.5 mL antifoam.

components.name: EDTA (disodium/ tetraacetic acid, chelator)
components.concentration: 15 g/L in trace-metal stock; 2 mL stock per L medium (0.03 g/L)
components.evidence: Trace metals solution (per liter): EDTA 15 g; ZnSO4·7H2O 4.5 g; CaCl2·2H2O 4.5 g; FeSO4·7H2O 3 g; H3BO3 1 g; MnCl2·4H2O 1 g; Na2MoO4·2H2O 0.4 g; CoCl2·6H2O 0.3 g; CuSO4·5H2O 0.1 g; KI 0.1 g (Delft medium, EasyClone).

media.base_medium: Mineral / DELFT medium (Verduyn-type defined yeast minimal medium), cited as ref 37 (Jensen et al. 2014, EasyClone, FEMS Yeast Res 14:238-248), with sodium acetate as sole carbon source (MMA variant)

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as ref 37 (Jensen 2014, EasyClone / DELFT medium)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch academic.oup.com/femsyr article for the Delft medium trace metals + vitamin solution composition

media.notes: MMA is the mineral/DELFT (Verduyn-type) medium of ref 37 with 27.3 g/L sodium acetate (= 20 g/L acetic acid) as sole carbon source, pH 6.0; the paper's main text gives only the bulk salts, acetate and stock-solution volumes, so all 10 trace metals and 7 vitamins were recovered from the cited EasyClone/Delft recipe. Organism is Yarrowia lipolytica grown aerobically (bioreactor DO maintained at 20%). Current_components previously listed only generic ion exchanges; calcium, all micronutrient metals and all vitamins were absent.
